input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

samples.output: <think>A blue sheet of construction paper sits on a wooden table alongside a white toilet paper roll and black scissors. Text lists needed materials like foil paper and markers. A person's arm reaches from the top right, initially near a foil box, then places their hand flat on the blue paper surface. A blue glue gun is visible on the left edge.</think><answer></answer>

[16-18] Two hands then display a cluster of markers with blue, pink, black, green, and purple caps over the paper near the stationary roll and scissors. [18-20] The markers vanish, and the hands hold the white toilet paper roll horizontally above the paper; the scissors are now positioned at the far left edge. [20-24] The hands manipulate and rotate the white cardboard tube to show its hollow circular opening while suspending it over the blue surface. [24-26] The

A right hand presses a white cardboard ring onto blue paper to secure a flower shape next to two stationary ones. Hands then hold a blue and red box labeled "ALCAN" and "25'" while unrolling a large sheet of shiny silver aluminum foil over the wooden table. The foil is extended across the frame, covering the blue paper and three white flower shapes. The hands then lift and move the crinkled foil sheet away from the camera view, revealing the dark blue construction paper and the three white cardboard flowers arranged on the surface. Finally, the "ALCAN" box is brought back into the frame from the right, extending the silver foil over the blue paper and hovering above the white cardboard flowers.

[78-80] A blue glue gun applies hot glue to the tip of a single white cardboard ring held above a blue paper surface with flower arrangements. [100-106] Hands extend a large sheet of shiny silver aluminum foil from a blue and red box labeled "ALCAN", positioning it horizontally over the blue construction paper to obscure the white cardboard flower shapes. [106-110] The foil is lowered onto the blue paper, covering the flowers. Two hands then press and smooth the crinkled silver foil across the surface on the wooden table, ensuring it lays flat over the craft.

[110-124] Two hands work on a wooden table, pressing and smoothing a large sheet of shiny silver aluminum foil over a dark blue piece of construction paper. The foil covers white cardboard flower shapes underneath as the palms flatten the crinkled metallic surface. The hands then fold the excess silver foil over the top edge of the blue paper. Fingers press the metallic strip firmly against the dark blue surface to secure the wrapping. Throughout the sequence, the hands repeatedly press down and smooth the foil along the top border, ensuring the material adheres tightly around the rectangular sheet.

From 124s to 136s, two hands press down firmly along the top edge of a dark blue rectangular sheet wrapped in silver aluminum foil. The fingers smooth the metallic material over the edge to secure the wrapping against the underlying craft project on the wooden table surface. The hands continue pressing and smoothing the silver foil along the top border of the dark blue construction paper. The fingers flatten the metallic material against the underlying surface to secure the wrap. The hands adjust slightly along the top border on the wooden table to flatten the foil against the paper surface. This ensures tight adhesion along the upper margin of the craft project. The left hand moves slightly while the right hand maintains pressure, and both hands remain positioned at the top border to ensure the foil adheres tightly to the sheet.

queries.question: White oval rings are arranged on the blue paper; how does the person secure these rings in place?

answers.text: The person secures the white oval rings, which form flower shapes, to the dark blue cardstock using a hot glue gun. Evidence shows a hand positioning a blue glue gun above the arranged white cardboard flowers on the blue sheet. The nozzle is aimed directly at the petals to apply adhesive, fixing the structures in place while loose rings remain piled separately on the wooden table.
A hot glue gun secures the white paper loops to the dark blue cardstock.
